minimum_sufficient_complexity_review.principle: Use the smallest route structure that can satisfy the frozen contract with the required proof strength.

display_plan.source: PM shallow projection from the full route tree

nodes.summary: Run startup interrogation, complete material intake handoff, complete PM-owned product-function architecture, freeze the contract, generate a candidate route tree, and freeze the checked root model as the first route version.
nodes.why_this_node_exists: Owns startup, product architecture, root contract, capability, and route-freeze obligations before implementation starts.
nodes.why_not_merged: Merging startup/contract freeze with implementation would let work begin before the acceptance floor and route proof are reviewed.
nodes.why_not_split: Split only when PM identifies independent startup, product, capability, or route-freeze subtrees with distinct evidence or failure recovery.

complexity_review.node_needed_reason: Startup and route-freeze gate owns independent hard gates that cannot be safely merged with implementation work.

nodes.summary: Review the current parent node's child subtree before entering child work.
nodes.why_this_node_exists: Owns parent-subtree review and continuation safety before child work proceeds.
nodes.why_not_merged: Parent-subtree review must remain distinct from child execution so stale or missing child structure is caught before dispatch.
nodes.why_not_split: Split only when the parent subtree has separately reviewable product, process, or evidence boundaries.

complexity_review.node_needed_reason: Parent subtree review is a distinct structural proof gate before child work.

nodes.summary: Verify invoked child skills followed their own workflow and quality standard before the parent node resumes.
nodes.why_this_node_exists: Owns selected child-skill standards when they add stricter workflow or evidence obligations.
nodes.why_not_merged: Child-skill conformance remains separate when skill standards require independent evidence before parent closure.
nodes.why_not_split: Skip or merge when no selected child skill adds a separate conformance obligation.

complexity_review.node_needed_reason: Conditional child-skill conformance is separate only when a selected child skill adds its own required gates.
complexity_review.merge_rejected_reason: May be skipped when no selected child skill requires separate conformance evidence.

nodes.summary: Execute bounded implementation chunks with declared verification.
nodes.why_this_node_exists: Owns implementation work that must produce evidence for mapped requirements.
nodes.why_not_merged: Implementation cannot close itself; verification and final ledger authority stay separate.
nodes.why_not_split: Split when implementation is too broad for one worker-ready packet or when different requirements need independent recovery.

complexity_review.node_needed_reason: Implementation is separated from verification so worker output cannot close itself.

nodes.summary: Run final verification, rebuild the PM-owned dynamic route-wide gate ledger from the current route, replay it backward with the human-like reviewer, then emit completion evidence only when unresolved count is zero.
nodes.why_this_node_exists: Owns final route-wide proof, requirement trace closure, and terminal backward replay.
nodes.why_not_merged: Final closure must remain independent from implementation so report-only or stale evidence cannot complete the route.
nodes.why_not_split: Split only when final verification, generated-resource lineage, or terminal replay require separate reviewed subtrees.

complexity_review.node_needed_reason: Final verification and ledger closure are distinct completion authority gates.
